sentences.zh: 安帝時，舉賢良方正，對策高第，為議郎。

translations.literal: Under Emperor An he was recommended as worthy, good, and upright; his policy response ranked high; he became gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor An he entered the worthy-and-upright examination with a top policy essay and became a gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 數陳得失，其言甚直。

translations.literal: He repeatedly set forth gains and losses; his words were very blunt.
translations.idiomatic: He often spoke bluntly on what was wrong with government.

sentences.zh: 出為武原令，時歲饑，輒開倉廩，活三千餘戶。

translations.literal: He went out as magistrate of Wuyuan; at that time the year was famine-stricken; he thereupon opened the granaries and saved over three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: As magistrate of Wuyuan during a famine he opened the granaries and kept more than three thousand households alive.

sentences.zh: 順帝時，遷冀州刺史。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Shun he was promoted to regional inspector of Ji province.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Shun he became regional inspector of Ji province.

sentences.zh: 故人為清河太守，章行部案其奸臧。

translations.literal: An old acquaintance was governor of Qinghe; when Zhang toured his department he investigated his corruption.
translations.idiomatic: An old friend was governor of Qinghe. On circuit Su Zhang investigated him for embezzlement.

sentences.zh: 乃請太守，為設酒肴，陳平生之好甚次。

translations.literal: Thereupon he invited the governor, set out wine and dishes for him, and laid out the closeness of their lifelong friendship in great detail.
translations.idiomatic: He invited the governor to a banquet and spoke warmly of their long friendship.

sentences.zh: 太守喜曰：『人皆有一天，我獨有二天。

translations.literal: The governor joyfully said, 'Other men all have one Heaven; I alone have two Heavens.'
translations.idiomatic: The governor exclaimed, 'Most men answer to one Heaven—I have two!'

sentences.zh: 』章曰：『今夕蘇孺文與故人飲者，私恩也；

translations.literal: Zhang said, 'Tonight Su Ruwen drinks with an old friend—that is private grace;'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang replied, 'Tonight Su Ruwen drinks with a friend—that is private kindness;'

sentences.zh: 明日冀州刺史案事者，公法也。

translations.literal: tomorrow the Ji provincial inspector who investigates affairs—that is public law.'
translations.idiomatic: tomorrow the inspector of Ji who reviews your case—that is the law of the state.'

sentences.zh: 』遂舉正其罪。

translations.literal: Thereupon he reported and rectified his crimes.
translations.idiomatic: He then filed charges and convicted him.

sentences.zh: 州境知章無私，望風畏肅。

translations.literal: The province's territory knew Zhang was without private bias; hearing the wind they feared and were awed.
translations.idiomatic: The whole province saw that Su Zhang showed no favoritism; word of it spread and men walked in fear.

sentences.zh: 換為并州刺史，以推折權豪，忤旨，坐免。

translations.literal: He was transferred to be regional inspector of Bing province; for pressing down the powerful magnates he offended the imperial intent and was dismissed for his offense.
translations.idiomatic: Transferred to Bing province, he crushed powerful families, crossed the throne, and was removed from office.

sentences.zh: 隱身鄉裏，不交當世。

translations.literal: He hid his person in his village and did not associate with the age.
translations.idiomatic: He withdrew to his village and shunned public life.

sentences.zh: 後徵為河南尹，不就。

translations.literal: Later he was summoned as governor of Henan; he did not take up the post.
translations.idiomatic: Later summoned as governor of Henan, he declined.

sentences.zh: 時天下日敝，民多悲苦，論者舉章有幹國才，朝廷不能復用，卒於家。

translations.literal: At that time the realm daily declined; the people were much sorrowful and bitter; discussers recommended Zhang as having talent to shore up the state, but the court could not employ him again; he died at home.
translations.idiomatic: The empire slid into decay and the people suffered. Critics said Su Zhang could still serve the state, but the court never recalled him, and he died at home.

sentences.zh: 兄曾孫不韋。

translations.literal: His elder brother's great-grandson was Buwei.
translations.idiomatic: The great-grandson of his elder brother was Su Buwei.

sentences.zh: 曾孫 不韋

translations.literal: Great-grandson Buwei (heading).
translations.idiomatic: Su Buwei, great-grandson (section title).

sentences.zh: 不韋字公先。

translations.literal: Buwei, courtesy name Gongxian.
translations.idiomatic: Su Buwei, courtesy name Gongxian.

sentences.zh: 父謙，初為郡督郵。

translations.literal: His father Qian, at first was commandery mail inspector.
translations.idiomatic: His father Qian began as commandery mail inspector.

sentences.zh: 時魏郡李暠為美陽令，與中常侍具瑗交通，貪暴為民患，前後監司畏其勢援，莫敢糾問。

translations.literal: At that time Li Gao of Wei commandery was magistrate of Meiyang; he communicated with the palace attendant Ju Yuan; greedy and violent, he became a scourge to the people; successive supervising officials feared his backing and none dared investigate.
translations.idiomatic: Li Gao of Wei was magistrate of Meiyang, in league with the eunuch Ju Yuan. He extorted and terrorized the people, and inspectors feared his patrons too much to touch him.

sentences.zh: 及謙至，部案得其臧，論輸左校。

translations.literal: When Qian arrived, he investigated the department and obtained his embezzlement; he was sentenced to labor at the left workshop.
translations.idiomatic: When Qian took the case, he proved the embezzlement and had Li Gao sentenced to the left convict workshop.

sentences.zh: 謙累遷至金城太守，去郡歸鄉裏。

translations.literal: Qian was promoted in stages to governor of Jincheng; he left the commandery and returned to his village.
translations.idiomatic: Qian rose to governor of Jincheng, then left office and went home.

sentences.zh: 漢法，免罷守令，自非詔徵，不得妄到京師。

translations.literal: By Han law, dismissed governors and magistrates, unless not summoned by edict, could not rashly reach the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Han law barred dismissed local officials from the capital unless the court summoned them.

sentences.zh: 而謙後私至洛陽，時暠為司隸校尉，收謙詰掠，死獄中，暠又因刑其屍，以報昔怨。

translations.literal: But Qian later privately went to Luoyang; at that time Gao was colonel of the metropolitan bureau; he seized Qian, interrogated and flogged him, and he died in prison; Gao thereupon also mutilated his corpse, to repay an old grudge.
translations.idiomatic: Qian went to Luoyang without leave. Gao, now colonel of the metropolitan bureau, arrested him, tortured him to death in jail, then mutilated the body to settle an old score.

sentences.zh: 不韋時年十八，徵詣公車，會謙見殺，不韋載喪歸鄉裏，瘞而不葬，仰天嘆曰：『伍子胥獨何人也！

translations.literal: Buwei at that time was eighteen years old; he was summoned to the public carriage [capital]; it happened that Qian was killed; Buwei carried the mourning back to his village, buried it but did not [formally] inter, looked up to Heaven and sighed, saying, 'Was Wu Zixu alone in being such a man!'
translations.idiomatic: Buwei was eighteen, bound for the capital on recommendation, when his father was murdered. He brought the body home, buried it provisionally, and cried to Heaven, 'Was Wu Zixu the only man who ever avenged a father!'

sentences.zh: 』乃藏母於武都山中，遂變名姓，盡以家財募劍客，邀暠於諸陵間，不克。

translations.literal: Thereupon he hid his mother in the mountains of Wudu, then changed his name, used all his family wealth to recruit swordsmen, and waylaid Gao among the imperial tombs, but did not succeed.
translations.idiomatic: He hid his mother in the Wudu hills, took a new name, spent his fortune on assassins, and ambushed Gao near the imperial tombs—without success.

sentences.zh: 會暠遷大司農，時右校芻廥在寺北垣下，不韋與親從兄弟潛入廥中，夜則鑿地，晝則逃伏。

translations.literal: It happened that Gao was promoted to minister of agriculture; at that time the right workshop's fodder granary was below the north wall of the office; Buwei with close cousins secretly entered the granary; at night they dug the earth, by day they fled and hid.
translations.idiomatic: When Gao became minister of agriculture, Buwei and kinsmen slipped into the fodder granary under the ministry's north wall, tunneling by night and hiding by day.

sentences.zh: 如此經月，遂得傍達暠之寢室，出其床下。

translations.literal: Thus for a month passed, and they were able to reach sideways to Gao's bedchamber and emerge below his bed.
translations.idiomatic: After a month the tunnel opened beneath Gao's bedchamber; they broke through under his couch.

sentences.zh: 值暠在廁，因殺其妾並及小兒，留書而去。

translations.literal: It happened that Gao was in the privy; thereupon they killed his concubine together with the small child, left a letter, and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Gao was in the privy. They killed his concubine and child, left a note, and fled.

sentences.zh: 暠大驚懼，乃布棘於室，以板籍地，一夕九徙，雖家人莫知其處。

translations.literal: Gao was greatly alarmed and fearful; thereupon he spread brambles in the room, used boards to cover the ground, moved nine times in one night—even his household did not know his whereabouts.
translations.idiomatic: Terrified, Gao lined his rooms with thorns, laid planks on the floor, and changed beds nine times a night—even his family never knew where he slept.

sentences.zh: 每出，輒劍戟隨身，壯士自衛，不韋知暠有備，乃日夜飛馳，徑到魏郡，掘其父阜冢，斷取阜頭，以祭父墳，又標之於市曰『李君遷父頭』。

translations.literal: Whenever he went out, he always had sword and halberd at his person, strong men guarding him; Buwei knew Gao was prepared; thereupon he day and night galloped swiftly, went straight to Wei commandery, dug up his father Gao's tomb, cut off and took Gao senior's head, used it to sacrifice at his father's grave, and also posted it in the market saying 'Lord Li moved his father's head.'
translations.idiomatic: Gao went nowhere without armed guards. Seeing that, Buwei raced to Wei commandery, opened old man Gao's grave, took the head, burned it on his father's tomb, and posted it in the market with a sign reading 'Li Gao moved his father's skull.'

sentences.zh: 暠匿不敢言，而自上退位，歸鄉裏，私掩塞冢槨。

translations.literal: Gao hid and dared not speak, but himself submitted to retire from office, returned to his village, and privately blocked up the tomb chamber.
translations.idiomatic: Gao kept silent, resigned, went home, and secretly sealed his father's violated tomb.

sentences.zh: 捕求不韋，歷歲不能得，憤恚感傷，發病歐血死。

translations.literal: They sought to capture Buwei; for years they could not obtain him; angry, resentful, and stricken, he fell ill vomiting blood and died.
translations.idiomatic: Years of failed manhunts left Gao consumed by rage; he spat blood and died.

sentences.zh: 不韋後遇赦還家，乃始改葬，行喪。

translations.literal: Buwei later met an amnesty and returned home; thereupon he began to re-bury and observe mourning.
translations.idiomatic: After an amnesty Buwei came home, reburied his father properly, and observed full mourning.

sentences.zh: 士大夫多譏其發掘冢墓，歸罪枯骨，不合古義，惟任城何休方之伍員。

translations.literal: Gentry mostly ridiculed him for digging open tombs and laying blame on dry bones, not matching ancient righteousness; only He Xiu of Rencheng compared him to Wu Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars mocked him for desecrating graves and attacking the dead, which classical ethics forbade. Only He Xiu of Rencheng likened him to Wu Zixu.

sentences.zh: 太原郭林宗聞而論之曰：『子胥雖雲逃命，而見用強吳，憑闔廬之威，因輕悍之眾，雪怨舊郢，曾不終朝，而但鞭墓戮屍，以舒其憤，竟無手刃後主之報。

translations.literal: Guo Linzong of Taiyuan heard and discoursed on him, saying, 'Though Zixu is said to have fled for his life, he was employed by mighty Wu, relied on Helü's might, used a light and fierce host, avenged his grudge on old Ying—not even a full morning passed—and yet only whipped the tomb and mutilated the corpse to ease his rage; in the end he had no repayment of cutting down the later lord with his own hand.'
translations.idiomatic: Guo Tai of Taiyuan disagreed: 'Wu Zixu fled to a strong Wu, rode King Helü's power, and sacked Ying in hours—yet he only flogged the dead king's corpse. He never struck the living ruler who had wronged him.'

sentences.zh: 豈如蘇子單特孑立，靡因靡資，強仇豪援，據位九卿，城闕天阻，宮府幽絕，埃塵所不能過，霧露所不能沾。

translations.literal: How does that compare to Master Su, alone and standing solitary, without reliance, without resources—his powerful foe had mighty backing, held rank among the nine ministers, the city gate-towers were blocked by Heaven, palace and offices were darkly cut off—dust could not pass, dew could not touch.'
translations.idiomatic: Su Buwei stood utterly alone. His enemy sat among the nine ministers with court and capital walled against common dust—nothing like Wu Zixu's war host.'

sentences.zh: 不韋毀身焦慮，出於百死，冒觸嚴禁，陷族禍門，雖不獲逞，為報已深。

translations.literal: Buwei destroyed his body and burned with anxiety, emerged from a hundred deaths, braved stern prohibitions, fell into the gate of clan disaster—though he did not succeed in carrying it out, the revenge was already deep.'
translations.idiomatic: He risked execution and extermination, broke every law, and still could not kill Gao in person—yet his vengeance ran deeper.'

sentences.zh: 況復分骸斷首，以毒生者，使暠懷忿結，不得其命，猶假手神靈以斃之也。

translations.literal: Moreover again splitting bones and cutting heads, to poison the living, made Gao harbor knotted resentment and not obtain his life—it was still borrowing the hand of spirits to destroy him.'
translations.idiomatic: He carved up the living household and left Gao a broken, haunted man—spirits might as well have struck the final blow.'

sentences.zh: 力惟匹夫，功隆千乘，比之於員，不以優乎？

translations.literal: His strength was only that of a common man; his achievement towered over a thousand chariots—compared to Yuan, is it not superior?'
translations.idiomatic: One man against a minister of state—his deed outweighs a princely host. Does he not surpass Wu Yuan?'

translations.literal: Question mark (stray duplicate punctuation in the received text).
translations.idiomatic: Duplicate closing punctuation in the manuscript; no separate sense.

sentences.zh: 議者於是貴之。

translations.literal: Discussers thereupon esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: After that, critics ranked Su Buwei the higher.

sentences.zh: 後太傅陳蕃辟，不應，為郡五官掾。

translations.literal: Later grand tutor Chen Fan summoned him; he did not respond; he became the commandery's clerk of the five bureaus.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Tutor Chen Fan later summoned him; he declined and served as a commandery clerk.

sentences.zh: 初，弘農張奐睦於蘇氏，而武威段颎與暠素善，後奐、颎有隙。

translations.literal: Earlier, Zhang Huan of Hongnong was at peace with the Su clan, while Duan Jiong of Wuwei had long been on good terms with Gao; later Huan and Jiong had a rift.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Huan of Hongnong favored the Su family; Duan Jiong of Wuwei was Li Gao's old friend. Later Huan and Jiong fell out.

sentences.zh: 及颎為司隸，以禮辟不韋，不韋懼之，稱病不詣。

translations.literal: When Jiong became colonel of the metropolitan bureau, with courtesy he summoned Buwei; Buwei feared him and claimed illness and did not go.
translations.idiomatic: When Jiong became colonel of the metropolitan bureau, he courteously summoned Buwei, who pleaded illness and stayed away.

sentences.zh: 颎既積憤於奐，因發怒，乃追咎不韋前報暠事，以為暠表治謙事，被報見誅，君命天也，而不韋仇之。

translations.literal: Jiong had long piled resentment against Huan; thereupon he gave vent to anger and traced back blame to Buwei's earlier revenge on Gao, holding that Gao had memorialized the handling of Qian's case, had been answered and executed—lordly command is Heaven's—and yet Buwei avenged himself on him.'
translations.idiomatic: Jiong already hated Huan; he now vented his rage on Buwei, arguing that Gao had lawfully prosecuted Qian and Heaven had approved the execution—yet Buwei had taken private revenge.'

sentences.zh: 又令長安男子告不韋多將賓客奪舅財物，遂使從事張賢等就家殺之。

translations.literal: He also ordered a man of Chang'an to accuse Buwei of often leading many guests to seize his uncle's goods; thereupon he sent adjutant Zhang Xian and others to his home to kill him.
translations.idiomatic: He had a Chang'an man accuse Buwei of robbing his uncle with a mob of retainers, then sent Adjutant Zhang Xian to kill him at home.

sentences.zh: 乃先以鴆與賢父曰：『若賢不得不韋，便可飲此。

translations.literal: Thereupon he first gave poison to Zhang Xian's father, saying, 'If Xian cannot obtain Buwei, then you may drink this.'
translations.idiomatic: He first handed Zhang Xian's father a cup of poison. 'If your son fails to kill Buwei, drink this.'

sentences.zh: 』賢到扶風，郡守使不韋奉謁迎賢，即時收執，並其一門六十餘人盡誅滅之，諸蘇以是衰破。

translations.literal: When Xian reached Fufeng, the governor had Buwei greet him with formal visit to welcome Xian; at once he seized and arrested him, and together with his one gate over sixty persons all were executed and extinguished; the various Sus for this reason declined and broke.
translations.idiomatic: At Fufeng the governor sent Buwei to meet Xian—then seized him and slaughtered more than sixty of his kin. The Su clan was destroyed.

sentences.zh: 乃段颎為陽球所誅，天下以為蘇氏之報焉。

translations.literal: Later Duan Jiong was executed by Yang Qiu; the realm thought it repayment for the Su clan.
translations.idiomatic: When Duan Jiong later died at Yang Qiu's hand, the empire called it heaven's answer for the Sus.

sentences.zh: 羊續字興祖，太山平陽人也。

translations.literal: Yang Xu, courtesy name Xingzu, was a man of Pingyang in Taishan.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xu, courtesy name Xingzu, came from Pingyang in Taishan.

sentences.zh: 其先七世二千石卿校，祖父侵，安帝時司隸校尉。

translations.literal: His ancestors for seven generations were two-thousand-shi ministers and colonels; his grandfather Qin, under Emperor An, was colonel of the metropolitan bureau.
translations.idiomatic: For seven generations his forebears had held two-thousand-shi rank. His grandfather Qin was colonel of the metropolitan bureau under Emperor An.

sentences.zh: 父儒，桓帝時為太常。

translations.literal: His father Ru, under Emperor Huan, was minister of ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: His father Ru was minister of ceremonies under Emperor Huan.

sentences.zh: 續以忠臣子孫拜郎中，去官後，辟大將軍竇武府。

translations.literal: Xu, as descendant of a loyal minister, was appointed gentleman of the palace; after leaving office he was summoned to grand general Dou Wu's bureau.
translations.idiomatic: As a descendant of a loyal official he became a gentleman of the palace, then joined Grand General Dou Wu's staff.

sentences.zh: 及武敗，坐黨事，禁錮十餘年，幽居守靜。

translations.literal: When Wu was defeated, he sat for the faction affair, was proscribed over ten years, and dwelt in seclusion keeping quiet.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Wu fell, he was caught in the faction proscription and spent more than a decade in seclusion.

sentences.zh: 及黨禁解，復辟太尉府，四遷為廬江太守。

translations.literal: When the faction prohibition was lifted, he was again summoned to the grand commandant's bureau; after four promotions he became governor of Lujiang.
translations.idiomatic: When the blacklist ended he joined the grand commandant's staff and after four promotions became governor of Lujiang.

sentences.zh: 後揚州黃巾賊攻舒，焚燒城郭，續發縣中男子二十以上，皆持兵勒陳，其小弱者，悉使負水灌火，會集數萬人，並執力戰，大破之，郡界平。

translations.literal: Later the Yangzhou Yellow Turban bandits attacked Shu, burned the walls; Xu drafted men of the county twenty and above, all holding weapons and arrayed in formation; the smaller and weaker—all were made to carry water to pour on the fire; they gathered several tens of thousands, all grasped weapons and fought hard, greatly defeated them, and the commandery border was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: When Yangzhou Yellow Turbans burned Shu, Yang Xu called up every man over twenty to fight, sent boys and the weak to haul water against the flames, and with tens of thousands routed the rebels and restored peace.

sentences.zh: 後安風賊戴風等作亂，續復擊破之，斬首三千餘級，生獲渠帥，其餘黨輩原為平民，賦與佃器，使就農業。

translations.literal: Later the Anfeng bandits Dai Feng and others made rebellion; Xu again struck and defeated them, cut off over three thousand heads, captured the chieftains alive; the rest of the faction he pardoned as common people, granted them tenant tools, and made them take up farming.
translations.idiomatic: He crushed Dai Feng's revolt at Anfeng, took three thousand heads, captured the leaders, paroled the followers as farmers, and gave them tools.

sentences.zh: 中平三年，江夏兵趙慈反叛，殺南陽太守秦頡，攻沒六縣，拜續為南陽太守。

translations.literal: In the third year of Zhongping, the Jiangxia soldier Zhao Ci rebelled, killed the governor of Nanyang Qin Jie, attacked and overthrew six counties; Xu was appointed governor of Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongping 3 Zhao Ci of Jiangxia killed Governor Qin Jie and overran six counties. Yang Xu was named governor of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 當入郡界，乃羸服間行，侍童子一人，觀歷縣邑，采問風謠，然後乃進。

translations.literal: When about to enter the commandery border, he wore worn clothes and traveled by byways, attended by one boy servant, observed and passed through counties and towns, gathered and asked local songs and rumors, and only then advanced.
translations.idiomatic: Before entering the commandery he traveled in disguise with a single boy, toured the towns, listened to rumor and song, then took office.

sentences.zh: 其令長貪挈，吏民良猾，悉逆知其狀，郡內驚竦，莫不震懾。

translations.literal: As for the magistrates' greed and grasping, clerks and people good and crafty—he all anticipated and knew their conditions; the commandery was startled and fearful; none were not awestruck.
translations.idiomatic: He learned which magistrates were corrupt and which clerks were honest or crooked before he arrived. The commandery trembled.

sentences.zh: 乃發兵與荊州刺史王敏共擊慈，斬之，獲首五千餘級，屬縣餘賊並詣續降，續為上言，宥其枝附。

translations.literal: Thereupon he raised troops and together with Jing provincial inspector Wang Min struck Ci, beheaded him, obtained over five thousand heads; the remaining bandits of the subordinate counties all came to Xu to surrender; Xu for them spoke upward and pardoned their branch adherents.
translations.idiomatic: He joined Inspector Wang Min of Jing province, killed Zhao Ci, took five thousand heads, and accepted the surrender of the remaining bandits, then petitioned to spare their followers.

sentences.zh: 賊既清平，乃班宣政令，候民病利，百姓歡服。

translations.literal: When the bandits were cleared and pacified, he thereupon promulgated government orders, watched the people's hardships and benefits, and the hundred surnames rejoiced and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: With the rebels gone he published fair regulations, asked after the people's welfare, and won their trust.

sentences.zh: 時，權豪之家多尚奢麗，續深疾之，常敝衣薄食，車馬羸敗。

translations.literal: At that time powerful magnate households mostly esteemed luxury and splendor; Xu deeply hated it; he always wore worn clothes and ate thin food; his carriage and horses were emaciated and ruined.
translations.idiomatic: While great families flaunted wealth, Yang Xu wore threadbare clothes, ate plain food, and drove broken-down carts in deliberate contrast.

sentences.zh: 府丞嘗獻其生魚，續受而懸於庭；

translations.literal: The assistant governor once presented him with live fish; Xu received them and hung them in the courtyard;
translations.idiomatic: His assistant once sent live fish; Yang Xu hung them in the courtyard;

sentences.zh: 丞後又進之，續乃出前所懸者以杜其意。

translations.literal: the assistant later presented them again; Xu thereupon brought out those formerly hung to stop his intent.
translations.idiomatic: when the gift came again, he pointed to the dried fish still hanging and refused the hint.

sentences.zh: 續妻後與子秘俱往郡舍，續閉門不內妻，自將秘行，其資藏惟有布衾、敝祗裯，鹽、麥數斛而已，顧敕秘曰：『吾自奉若此，何以資爾母乎？

translations.literal: Xu's wife later together with his son Mi both went to the commandery residence; Xu closed the door and would not admit his wife; he personally led Mi on a tour; his stored goods had only a cloth quilt, worn lined jacket, several hu of salt and wheat—that was all; he turned and ordered Mi, saying, 'I supply myself thus—how can I supply your mother?'
translations.idiomatic: When his wife and son Mi visited headquarters, he barred the door to his wife, showed Mi his quarters—only a cotton quilt, a patched coat, a few measures of salt and grain—and said, 'I live like this; how could I support your mother?'

sentences.zh: 』使與母俱歸。

translations.literal: He made him return together with his mother.
translations.idiomatic: He sent them home together.

sentences.zh: 六年，靈帝欲以續為太尉。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Emperor Ling wished to make Xu grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year Emperor Ling wished to appoint Yang Xu grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 時拜三公者，皆輸東園禮錢千萬，令中使督之，名為『左騶』。

translations.literal: At that time those appointed to the three dukes all paid the Eastern Park courtesy cash of ten million, ordered inner attendants to supervise it, named 'left outrider.'
translations.idiomatic: Appointees to the three dukes had to pay ten million cash to the Eastern Park treasury under eunuch collectors—the so-called 'left outrider' fee.

sentences.zh: 其所之往，輒迎致禮敬，厚加贈賂。

translations.literal: Wherever he went, they always came to meet him with ritual respect and greatly added gifts and bribes.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever the collectors followed him, local officials greeted them with honors and rich bribes.

sentences.zh: 續乃坐使人於單席，舉缊袍以示之，曰：『臣之所資，惟斯而已。

translations.literal: Xu thereupon made the messenger sit on a single mat, raised a padded robe of tangled silk to show him, and said, 'What your servant relies on is only this.'
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xu seated the eunuch on a straw mat and held up his worn padded robe. 'This is all I own,' he said.

sentences.zh: 』左騶白之，帝不悅，以此故不登公位。

translations.literal: The left outrider reported it; the emperor was displeased; for this reason he did not ascend to a ducal post.
translations.idiomatic: The collector reported back; the emperor took offense, and Yang Xu never received a seat among the three dukes.

sentences.zh: 而徵為太常，未及行，會病卒，時年四十八。

translations.literal: But he was summoned as minister of ceremonies; before he could go, he happened to die of illness, aged forty-eight.
translations.idiomatic: He was recalled as minister of ceremonies but died at forty-eight before taking up the post.

sentences.zh: 遺言薄斂，不受赗遺。

translations.literal: He left instructions for a thin burial and not to accept funeral gifts.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered a plain funeral and refused burial gifts.

sentences.zh: 舊典，二千石卒官賻百萬，府丞焦儉遵續先意，一無所受。

translations.literal: By old statute, when a two-thousand-shi official died in office the condolence payment was a million cash; assistant governor Jiao Jian followed Xu's prior intent and accepted nothing.
translations.idiomatic: Statute allowed a million cash for a fallen two-thousand-shi official. Assistant Governor Jiao Jian honored Xu's wishes and refused every coin.

sentences.zh: 詔書褒美，敕太山太守以府賻錢賜續家雲。

translations.literal: An edict praised his beauty of conduct and ordered the governor of Taishan to grant the office condolence money to Xu's family—it is said.
translations.idiomatic: An edict praised him and directed the governor of Taishan to pay the funeral subsidy to his family.

sentences.zh: 賈琮字孟堅，東郡聊城人也。

translations.literal: Jia Cong, courtesy name Mengjian, was a man of Liaocheng in Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Cong, courtesy name Mengjian, came from Liaocheng in Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 舉孝廉，再遷為京令，有政理跡。

translations.literal: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, after two promotions he became magistrate of Jing, and had traces of good government.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, he rose to magistrate of Jing with a record of sound administration.

sentences.zh: 舊交阯土多珍產，明璣、翠羽、犀、象、玳瑁、異香、美木之屬，莫不自出。

translations.literal: Formerly the land of Jiaozhi had many precious products—bright pearls, kingfisher feathers, rhinoceros, elephant, tortoiseshell, exotic incense, fine woods of such kinds—none did not come forth of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Jiaozhi once teemed with pearls, feathers, ivory, rhino horn, tortoiseshell, rare incense, and fine timber, all produced locally.

sentences.zh: 前後刺史率多無情行，上承權貴，下積私賂，財計盈給，輒復求見遷代，故吏民怨叛。

translations.literal: Former and later regional inspectors mostly lacked honest conduct; above they flattered the powerful and noble, below they piled up private bribes; when their financial accounts were full, they always again sought audience for transfer and replacement; therefore officials and people resented and rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector after inspector lined his pockets, curried favor at court, extorted bribes below, and left for a new post once rich—so the people rose in fury.

sentences.zh: 中平元年，交阯屯兵反，執刺史及合浦太守，自稱『柱天將軍』。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhongping, the garrison troops of Jiaozhi rebelled, seized the regional inspector and the governor of Hepu, and styled themselves 'pillar-of-heaven general.'
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongping 1 the Jiaozhi garrison mutinied, seized the inspector and the governor of Hepu, and proclaimed a 'pillar-of-heaven general.'

sentences.zh: 靈帝特敕三府精選能吏，有司舉琮為交阯刺史。

translations.literal: Emperor Ling specially ordered the three offices to pick able officials with care; the responsible offices recommended Cong as regional inspector of Jiaozhi.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling ordered the three bureaus to nominate able men, and Jia Cong was named inspector of Jiaozhi.

sentences.zh: 琮到部，訊其反狀，鹹言賦斂過重，百姓莫不空單，京師遙遠，告冤無所，民不聊生，故聚為盜賊。

translations.literal: When Cong reached his department, he inquired into the conditions of their rebellion; all said levies were too heavy, the hundred surnames were all empty and alone, the capital was distant, there was nowhere to cry injustice, the people could not sustain life—therefore they gathered and became bandits.
translations.idiomatic: On arrival he asked why they had revolted. All blamed crushing taxes, destitution, and no redress so far from the capital—so men had turned bandit.

sentences.zh: 琮即移書告示，各使安其資業，招撫荒散，蠲復徭役，誅斬渠帥為大害者，簡選良吏試守諸縣，歲間蕩定，百姓以安。

translations.literal: Cong at once shifted documents and proclaimed notice, each making them secure in their property and occupations, summoned and comforted the scattered in wasteland, remitted and restored corvée service, executed and beheaded ringleaders who did great harm, selected and picked good clerks to probationarily guard the various counties; within a year it was swept and settled, and the hundred surnames were thereby secure.
translations.idiomatic: He issued orders letting people keep their livelihoods, brought refugees in, cut corvée, executed the worst chiefs, and appointed honest men to acting magistracies. Within a year the region was quiet.

sentences.zh: 巷路為之歌曰：『賈父來晚，使我先反；

translations.literal: The lanes and roads for him sang: 'Father Jia came late, making us first rebel;'
translations.idiomatic: A song ran in the streets: 'If Father Jia had come sooner, we would not have risen first;'

sentences.zh: 今見清平，吏不敢飯。

translations.literal: now we see purity and peace; clerks do not dare take meals [bribes].'
translations.idiomatic: now that the rule is clean, clerks dare not even take a meal bribe.'

sentences.zh: 』在事三年，為十三州最，徵拜議郎。

translations.literal: After three years in office, he was foremost among the thirteen provinces; he was summoned and appointed gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: After three years he ranked first among the thirteen provinces and was recalled as gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 時，黃巾新破，兵兇之後，郡縣重斂，因緣生奸。

translations.literal: At that time the Yellow Turbans had newly been broken; after the violence of arms, commanderies and counties heavily levied, and by opportunity wickedness was born.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Turbans had just been crushed; counties were taxing heavily in the wake of war, and corruption flourished.

sentences.zh: 詔書沙汰刺史、二千石，更選清能吏，乃以琮為冀州刺史。

translations.literal: An edict sifted and washed regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials, selecting anew pure and capable clerks; thereupon Cong was made regional inspector of Ji province.
translations.idiomatic: An edict purged inspectors and governors and named honest men in their place; Jia Cong became inspector of Ji province.

sentences.zh: 舊典，傳車驂駕，垂赤帷裳，迎於州界。

translations.literal: By old statute, the relay carriage was three-horse hitched, with red curtain-skirts hanging down; they welcomed at the province border.
translations.idiomatic: By custom a three-horse relay coach with red curtains met the inspector at the border.

sentences.zh: 及琮之部，升車言曰：『刺史當遠視廣聽，糾察美惡，何有反垂帷裳以自掩塞乎？

translations.literal: When Cong reached his department, he mounted the carriage and said, 'A regional inspector ought to look far and listen broadly, investigate good and evil—how can there be hanging curtain-skirts to block and seal oneself instead?'
translations.idiomatic: At the border Cong mounted his carriage and said, 'An inspector must see and hear everything. Why hide behind curtains?'

sentences.zh: 』乃命禦者褰之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he ordered the driver to raise them.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the driver to strip them away.

sentences.zh: 百城聞風，自然竦震。

translations.literal: The hundred cities heard the wind and naturally stood in awe and trembled.
translations.idiomatic: Word spread through the commanderies and every magistrate trembled.

sentences.zh: 其諸臧過者，望風解印綬去，惟癭陶長濟陰董昭、觀津長梁國黃就當官待琮，於是州界翕然。

translations.literal: Those of them who were corrupt and at fault, hearing the wind, removed seals and ribbons and departed; only the magistrate of Yingtao, Dong Zhao of Jiyin, and the magistrate of Guanjin, Huang Jiu of Liang state, remained in office awaiting Cong; thereupon the province border was of one accord.
translations.idiomatic: Corrupt magistrates fled before he arrived. Only Dong Zhao of Yingtao and Huang Jiu of Guanjin stayed at their posts to meet him. The province settled into order.

sentences.zh: 靈帝崩，大將軍何進表琮為度遼將軍，卒於官。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ling died, grand general He Jin memorialized Cong as general who crosses the Liao; he died in office.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Ling died, Grand General He Jin named him general who crosses the Liao; he died in that post.

sentences.zh: 陸康字季寧，吳郡吳人也。

translations.literal: Lu Kang, courtesy name Jining, was a man of Wu in Wu commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang, courtesy name Jining, came from Wu in Wu commandery.

sentences.zh: 祖父續，在《獨行傳》。

translations.literal: His grandfather Xu is in the 'Lone conduct' biographies.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather Lu Xu has a biography in the chapter on solitary integrity.

sentences.zh: 父褒，有誌操，連徵不至。

translations.literal: His father Bao had aims and integrity; repeatedly summoned, he did not arrive.
translations.idiomatic: His father Bao was a man of principle who repeatedly refused summons.

sentences.zh: 康少仕郡，以義烈稱，刺史臧旻舉為茂才，除高成令。

translations.literal: Kang in youth served the commandery and was famed for righteous ardor; regional inspector Zang Min recommended him as flourishing talent; he was appointed magistrate of Gaocheng.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang served his commandery young and was known for integrity. Inspector Zang Min recommended him as flourishing talent, and he became magistrate of Gaocheng.

sentences.zh: 縣在邊垂，令戶一人具弓弩以備不虞，不得行來。

translations.literal: The county was on the frontier edge; it ordered each household one man to equip bow and crossbow to prepare for the unforeseen; they could not come and go freely.
translations.idiomatic: The frontier county required one man per household armed with bow and crossbow; travel was restricted.

sentences.zh: 長吏新到，輒發民繕修城郭。

translations.literal: When senior clerks newly arrived, they always drafted the people to repair and build walls and moats.
translations.idiomatic: New magistrates habitually drafted the people to repair the walls.

sentences.zh: 康至，皆罷遣，百姓大悅。

translations.literal: When Kang arrived, he abolished and dismissed all; the hundred surnames were greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang ended both practices, and the people rejoiced.

sentences.zh: 以恩信為治，寇盜亦息，州郡表上其狀。

translations.literal: He used kindness and trustworthiness in rule; raiders and bandits also ceased; the province and commandery memorialized upward his condition.
translations.idiomatic: He ruled with kindness and good faith; banditry subsided, and provincial authorities reported his success.

sentences.zh: 光和元年，遷武陵太守，轉守桂陽、樂安二郡，所在稱之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Guanghe, he was promoted to governor of Wuling, transferred to guard the two commanderies Guilin and Le'an; wherever he was, he was praised.
translations.idiomatic: In Guanghe 1 he became governor of Wuling, then Guiyang and Le'an, earning praise in each post.

sentences.zh: 時，靈帝欲鑄銅人，而國用不足，乃詔調民田，畝斂十錢。

translations.literal: At that time Emperor Ling wished to cast bronze men, but state expenditure was insufficient; thereupon he edicted levying the people's fields, ten cash per mu.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling planned bronze statues but lacked funds, so he ordered a tax of ten cash per mu of farmland.

sentences.zh: 而比水旱傷稼，百姓貧苦。

translations.literal: Moreover recently flood and drought had injured the crops; the hundred surnames were poor and bitter.
translations.idiomatic: Floods and drought had ruined the harvests; the people were destitute.

sentences.zh: 康上疏諫曰：『臣聞先王治世，貴在愛民。

translations.literal: Kang submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying, 'Your servant has heard that the former kings' governing the age prized loving the people.'
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang memorialized: 'The ancient kings put love for the people first.'

sentences.zh: 省徭輕賦，以寧天下，除煩就約，以崇簡易，故萬姓從化，靈物應德。

translations.literal: Reduce corvée and lighten levies, thereby to pacify the realm; remove vexation and approach simplicity, thereby to honor the easy and simple; therefore the myriad surnames follow transformation, and numinous creatures respond to virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: They cut labor service and taxes, simplified government, and won the people—spirits and omens answered their virtue.'

sentences.zh: 末世衰主，窮奢極侈，造作無端，興制非一，勞割自下，以從茍欲，故黎民吁嗟，陰陽感動。

translations.literal: Decaying lords of a latter age, exhaustively extravagant and extreme in splendor, make and construct without limit, raise institutions not of one kind, labor and cut from below to follow base desires; therefore the black-haired people sigh, and yin and yang are moved.'
translations.idiomatic: Late rulers ruined themselves with endless projects and taxes to satisfy whim, and the people groaned—Heaven and earth responded with disaster.'

sentences.zh: 陛下聖德承天，當隆盛化，而卒被詔書，畝斂田錢，鑄作銅人，伏讀惆悵，悼心失圖。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's sagely virtue receives from Heaven; you ought to elevate flourishing transformation; yet suddenly receive an edict, per-mu levy field cash, cast and make bronze men—your servant reads prostrate and is melancholy, grieves in heart and loses plans.'
translations.idiomatic: Your virtue matches Heaven's; you should bring a golden age—yet now a tax on every mu and bronze giants. I read the edict with grief and lose heart.'

sentences.zh: 夫十一而稅，周謂之徹。

translations.literal: As for one-tenth taxation, Zhou called it che.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou called a one-tenth land tax the che levy.'

sentences.zh: 徹者通也，言其法度可通萬世而行也。

translations.literal: Che means penetrate; it says its standards can penetrate myriad generations and be practiced.'
translations.idiomatic: Che means universal—that law was fit for all ages.'

sentences.zh: 故魯宣稅畝，而蝝災自生；

translations.literal: Therefore Duke Xuan of Lu taxed by the mu, and locust larvae disaster arose of itself;'
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Xuan of Lu taxed by the mu, locust plagues followed;'

sentences.zh: 哀公增賦，而孔子非之。

translations.literal: Duke Ai increased levies, and Confucius condemned it.'
translations.idiomatic: when Duke Ai raised taxes, Confucius condemned him.'

sentences.zh: 豈有聚奪民物，以營無用之銅人；

translations.literal: How can there be gathering and seizing the people's goods to build useless bronze men;'
translations.idiomatic: How can you strip the people to cast useless statues;'

sentences.zh: 捐舍聖戒，自蹈亡王之法哉！

translations.literal: cast aside the sage's warnings and yourself tread the methods of perished kings!'
translations.idiomatic: discard the sages' warnings and repeat the errors of fallen dynasties!'

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「君舉必書，書而不法，後世何述焉？

translations.literal: The tradition says: "The ruler's acts must be recorded; if recorded and not according to law, what will later ages narrate?'"
translations.idiomatic: The classic says: "A ruler's deeds are chronicled; if they are lawless, what can later ages praise?'"

sentences.zh: 」陛下宜留神省察，改敝從善，以塞兆民怨恨之望。

translations.literal: Your Majesty ought to keep mind and examine, reform the bad and follow the good, thereby to stop the myriad people's resentful hopes.'
translations.idiomatic: Reflect on this, turn from error to good, and answer the people's plea.'

sentences.zh: 』書奏，內幸因此譖康援引亡國，以譬聖明，大不敬，檻車徵詣廷尉。

translations.literal: When the memorial was presented, the inner favorites for this reason slandered Kang for citing fallen states to compare the sage brightness—great irreverence—and a caged cart summoned him to the commandant of justice.
translations.idiomatic: The eunuchs charged him with likening the emperor to doomed tyrants—a capital offense—and had him hauled to the commandant of justice in a prison cart.

sentences.zh: 待御史劉岱典考其事，岱為表陳解釋，免歸田里。

translations.literal: Attendant censor Liu Dai presided over examining the affair; Dai for him memorialized explaining and clarifying; he was dismissed and returned to field and village.
translations.idiomatic: Attendant Censor Liu Dai reviewed the case, filed a defense, and Lu Kang was cleared and sent home.

sentences.zh: 復徵拜議郎。

translations.literal: Again he was summoned and appointed gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: He was later recalled as gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 會廬江賊黃穰等與江夏蠻連結十餘萬人，攻沒四縣，拜康廬江太守。

translations.literal: It happened that the Lujiang bandits Huang Rang and others linked with Jiangxia Man over a hundred thousand persons, attacked and overthrew four counties; Kang was appointed governor of Lujiang.
translations.idiomatic: When Huang Rang of Lujiang and over a hundred thousand Jiangxia tribesmen overran four counties, Lu Kang was named governor of Lujiang.

sentences.zh: 康申明賞罰，擊破穰等，餘黨悉降。

translations.literal: Kang clarified rewards and punishments, struck and defeated Rang and the rest; the remaining faction all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: He made rewards and punishments clear, crushed Huang Rang, and accepted the surrender of the rest.

sentences.zh: 帝嘉其功，拜康孫尚為郎中。

translations.literal: The emperor praised his merit and appointed Kang's grandson Shang as gentleman of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor honored his service by appointing his grandson Lu Shang as gentleman of the palace.

sentences.zh: 獻帝即位，天下大亂，康蒙險遣孝廉計吏奉貢朝廷，詔書策勞，加忠義將軍，秩中二千石。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xian ascended the throne, the realm was in great chaos; Kang braving danger sent filial-incorrupt and accounting clerks to present tribute to the court; an edict with bamboo slip lauded his labor, added general of loyalty and righteousness, rank of median two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Xian's accession, while the realm collapsed, Lu Kang sent tribute through perilous roads. The court thanked him with edict, named him general of loyalty and righteousness at median two-thousand-shi rank.

sentences.zh: 時袁術屯兵壽春，部曲饑餓，遣使求委輸兵甲。

translations.literal: At that time Yuan Shu garrisoned troops at Shouchun; his retainers were hungry; he sent envoys seeking delivery of weapons and armor.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shu was camped at Shouchun with starving troops; he sent to Lu Kang for supplies and arms.

sentences.zh: 康以其叛逆，閉門不通，內修戰備，將以禦之。

translations.literal: Kang, because he was rebellious and traitorous, closed the gates and did not communicate; inwardly he repaired war preparations, intending thereby to resist him.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang treated him as a rebel, barred his envoys, and prepared the city's defenses.

sentences.zh: 術大怒，遣其將孫策政康，圍城數重。

translations.literal: Shu was greatly angry and sent his general Sun Ce to attack Kang; the city was surrounded in several layers. (The character in the text is written as in 'government' but means 'attack.')
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Shu was furious and sent Sun Ce to besiege Lu Kang; camp after camp ringed the city.

sentences.zh: 康固守，吏士有先受休假者，皆遁伏還赴，暮夜緣城而入。

translations.literal: Kang firmly defended; clerks and soldiers who had previously received leave and furlough all fled hiding and returned to answer the call; at dusk and night they climbed the walls and entered.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang held the city. Men on leave stole back by night and scaled the walls to rejoin the defense.

sentences.zh: 受敵二年，城陷。

translations.literal: Receiving the enemy two years, the city fell.
translations.idiomatic: After two years the city fell.

sentences.zh: 月餘，發病卒，年七十。

translations.literal: After more than a month he fell ill and died, aged seventy.
translations.idiomatic: He fell ill and died a month later at seventy.

sentences.zh: 宗族百餘人，遭離饑厄，死者將半。

translations.literal: Clan and lineage over a hundred persons encountered separation and famine hardship; nearly half died.
translations.idiomatic: Of more than a hundred kinsmen caught in the siege and famine, nearly half perished.

sentences.zh: 朝廷湣其守節，拜子俊為郎中。

translations.literal: The court pitied his guarding integrity and appointed his son Jun as gentleman of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The court honored his loyalty by appointing his son Lu Jun as gentleman of the palace.

sentences.zh: 少子績，仕吳為郁林太守，博學善政，見稱當時。

translations.literal: His younger son Ji served Wu as governor of Yulin, broadly learned and good in government, praised in his time.
translations.idiomatic: His youngest son Lu Ji became governor of Yulin under Wu, famed for learning and good rule.

sentences.zh: 幼年曾謁袁術，懷橘墮地者也，有名稱。

translations.literal: In youth he once visited Yuan Shu—he was the one who hid tangerines in his bosom and dropped them on the ground—he had a famous reputation.
translations.idiomatic: As a boy he once visited Yuan Shu—the child who hid oranges in his sleeve and became famous for it.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：伋牧朔藩，信立童昏。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Ji shepherded the Shuofang frontier; trust was established with children.
translations.idiomatic: Encomium: Guo Ji governed the northern frontier and kept faith even with the children along his route.

sentences.zh: 詩守南楚，民作謠言。

translations.literal: Shi guarded southern Chu; the people made rhyming words.
translations.idiomatic: Du Shi governed southern Chu, and the people sang his praise.

sentences.zh: 奮馳單乘，堪駕毀轅。

translations.literal: Fen galloped in a single carriage; Kan drove a broken axle-shaft.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Fen departed Guzang without baggage; Zhang Kan left Chengdu in a cart with a broken axle.

sentences.zh: 範得其朋，堂任良肱。

translations.literal: Fan obtained his companions; Tang employed good forearms [i.e. capable assistants].
translations.idiomatic: Lian Fan chose true friends; Wang Tang relied on able deputies.

sentences.zh: 二蘇勁烈，羊、賈廉能。

translations.literal: The two Sus were keen and fierce; Yang and Jia were pure and capable.
translations.idiomatic: The two Sus were stern integrity; Yang Xu and Jia Cong were clean hands.

sentences.zh: 季寧拒策，城隕沖朋。

translations.literal: Jining resisted Ce; the city fell and kin were lost.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Kang defied Sun Ce; the city fell and his clan was shattered.
